mechanism_narrative: A sustained low level of the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly increasing present valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 8.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration and declining consumer spending power, which reduces corporate earnings forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the conditional return distribution shows no significant left skew.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion, which drives higher corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.05, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth outpaces monetary policy tightening, indicating resilient supply-side capacity that supports corporate margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal predicts negative 5-day returns in > 55% of observations during tightening cycles, or the out-of-sample hit rate drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index values above 215.0 reflect entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in > 65% of high-inflation trigger months, or the signal predictive power vanishes when controlling for Fed funds rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth combined with a stable unemployment rate confirms genuine labor market expansion without slack, which sustains wage-driven consumption and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction effect yields a Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or the joint condition fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth provides a direct measure of aggregate economic expansion, which translates into higher corporate sales and earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day returns in > 55% of observations, or the Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 90.0 indicates a severe contraction in manufacturing and industrial output, which reduces corporate profitability and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the conditional return distribution shows no significant negative skew during low-production periods.
